title: Low cloud-cover has helped mute warming for the last 47 years, but models predict this will change
excerpt: A recent study, published in Geophysical Research Letters, combines historical satellite observations with atmospheric reanalysis data to construct a multidecade record of low-cloud cover in the hope of improving current climate models.
content_html:
A recent study, published in Geophysical Research Letters, combines historical satellite observations with atmospheric reanalysis data to construct a multidecade record of low-cloud cover in the hope of improving current climate models.

The study reveals that low-cloud cover has historically had a protective effect against warming, but climate models still show a reduced effect from low-lying clouds in the future, along with increasing climate sensitivity.

A cloudy understanding of low clouds

Scientists know low clouds reflect sunlight back into space and that this can result in a cooling effect. However, there is still uncertainty about how these clouds are affected by a warming planet. Most climate models expect low clouds over tropical oceans to decline as the planet warms and further amplify that warming, but how much they might decline and the degree to which they will affect warming vary among models.

Thus far, satellite cloud records have been fragmented because instruments, calibrations and cloud definitions have changed over time. This has prevented a reliable direct estimate of how low clouds have changed during the modern warming period, making it difficult to model cloud effects in future projections.

Some studies have inferred low-cloud feedback from the relationship between clouds and cloud-controlling factors (CCFs), then combined that with CMIP phase 6 (CMIP6) model projections. These generally result in positive low-cloud cover (LCC) feedback values with varying magnitudes, meaning they have a warming effect (while negative LCC feedback values mean a cooling effect).

The authors of the new study say present-day low-cloud feedback is not often discussed, but prior studies showed that inferred tropical low-cloud feedback could be three to four times smaller when CCF changes are taken from the historical record instead of CMIP6 projections. They say this casts doubt on the plausibility of future cloud feedback estimates based on idealized warming scenarios.

Satellite data provide some historical clarity

The team combined observations from 13 satellites with atmospheric reanalysis data in an attempt to reconstruct past cloud conditions from 1979 to 2025. To do this, they developed an anomaly-based framework that minimizes platform- and algorithm-dependent biases and provides more consistent comparisons across satellites.

Their results showed that tropical low-cloud cover increased by about 0.18% per decade from 1979 to 2025. This rise mainly consisted of stratocumulus clouds, which are especially effective at reflecting sunlight. They also estimated that the rise in cloud cover produced a negative low-cloud feedback of about −0.79 watts per square meter per degree Celsius, a cooling influence that contributed to slowed warming in the past few decades.

"This overall LCC increase has occurred alongside a ∼1 K rise in global mean surface temperature, linking cloud changes to the observed warming trend. The CCF decomposition indicates that the positive LCC trend is primarily driven by increases in estimated inversion strength (EIS) and surface wind speed contributions (0.14 ± 0.04%/decade and 0.07 ± 0.02%/decade, respectively), while the SST contribution (−0.08 ± 0.02%/decade) acts in the opposite direction and partly offsets the increase," the study authors write.

Combining historical data with climate models

Although historical warming shows an association with a negative low-cloud feedback that provides a welcome cooling effect, the team found that when models matched this historical data, the news was still not good. When the team compared the observed historical record with simulations from CMIP6 climate models, they found that the models that best matched observed cloud patterns and trends projected the strongest positive future low-cloud feedback and higher climate sensitivity.

In other idealized climate-model experiments that did not match historical observations, the average response showed low-cloud losses and extra warming. This appears to be because historical observations indicate more reflective clouds, particularly in the East Pacific, while idealized warming patterns favored less reflective clouds.

Most models predicted more heating in the eastern Pacific, similar to western Pacific regions, but observed patterns instead show that a mix of hot and cool air has allowed for the formation of low-cloud cover. However, scientists predict that further heating and changes in atmospheric circulation will eventually reverse this protective effect.

While this study contributes cloud feedback estimates that could improve projections of future temperature rise, there is still room for improvement in these models. The authors write, "Improving the representation of SST trends in coupled climate models—and better constraining their forced versus internal components—will therefore be critical for narrowing uncertainties in cloud feedbacks and climate sensitivity."

Written for you by our author Krystal Kasal, edited by Lisa Lock, and fact-checked and reviewed by Robert Egan—this article is the result of careful human work. We rely on readers like you to keep independent science journalism alive. If this reporting matters to you, please consider a donation (especially monthly). blocks.html_en: A recent study, published in Geophysical Research Letters, combines historical satellite observations with atmospheric reanalysis data to construct a multidecade record of low-cloud cover in the hope of improving current climate models.

sentences.en: A recent study, published in Geophysical Research Letters, combines historical satellite observations with atmospheric reanalysis data to construct a multidecade record of low-cloud cover in the hope of improving current climate models.
sentences.zh: 最近发表在《地球物理研究快报》上的一项研究将历史卫星观测与大气再分析数据结合起来，构建了数十年的低云量记录，希望改进当前的气候模型。

blocks.html_en: The study reveals that low-cloud cover has historically had a protective effect against warming, but climate models still show a reduced effect from low-lying clouds in the future, along with increasing climate sensitivity.

sentences.en: The study reveals that low-cloud cover has historically had a protective effect against warming, but climate models still show a reduced effect from low-lying clouds in the future, along with increasing climate sensitivity.
sentences.zh: 研究表明，低云量历史上对变暖具有保护作用，但气候模型仍然显示，未来低云量的影响会减弱，而且气候敏感性也会增加。

blocks.html_en: A cloudy understanding of low clouds

sentences.en: A cloudy understanding of low clouds
sentences.zh: 对低云的多云理解

blocks.html_en: Scientists know low clouds reflect sunlight back into space and that this can result in a cooling effect. However, there is still uncertainty about how these clouds are affected by a warming planet. Most climate models expect low clouds over tropical oceans to decline as the planet warms and further amplify that warming, but how much they might decline and the degree to which they will affect warming vary among models.

sentences.en: Scientists know low clouds reflect sunlight back into space and that this can result in a cooling effect.
sentences.zh: 科学家们知道低云会将阳光反射回太空，这会导致冷却效果。

sentences.en: However, there is still uncertainty about how these clouds are affected by a warming planet.
sentences.zh: 然而，这些云如何受到地球变暖的影响仍存在不确定性。

sentences.en: Most climate models expect low clouds over tropical oceans to decline as the planet warms and further amplify that warming, but how much they might decline and the degree to which they will affect warming vary among models.
sentences.zh: 大多数气候模型预计，随着地球变暖，热带海洋上空的低云会减少，并进一步加剧变暖，但它们可能减少的程度以及对变暖的影响程度因模型而异。

blocks.html_en: Thus far, satellite cloud records have been fragmented because instruments, calibrations and cloud definitions have changed over time. This has prevented a reliable direct estimate of how low clouds have changed during the modern warming period, making it difficult to model cloud effects in future projections.

sentences.en: Thus far, satellite cloud records have been fragmented because instruments, calibrations and cloud definitions have changed over time.
sentences.zh: 到目前为止，卫星云记录已经支离破碎，因为仪器、校准和云定义随着时间的推移而发生了变化。

sentences.en: This has prevented a reliable direct estimate of how low clouds have changed during the modern warming period, making it difficult to model cloud effects in future projections.
sentences.zh: 这阻碍了对现代变暖时期低云变化程度的可靠直接估计，使得在未来预测中难以对云效应进行建模。

blocks.html_en: Some studies have inferred low-cloud feedback from the relationship between clouds and cloud-controlling factors (CCFs), then combined that with CMIP phase 6 (CMIP6) model projections. These generally result in positive low-cloud cover (LCC) feedback values with varying magnitudes, meaning they have a warming effect (while negative LCC feedback values mean a cooling effect).

sentences.en: Some studies have inferred low-cloud feedback from the relationship between clouds and cloud-controlling factors (CCFs), then combined that with CMIP phase 6 (CMIP6) model projections.
sentences.zh: 一些研究根据云与云控制因素 (CCF) 之间的关系推断出低云反馈，然后将其与 CMIP 第 6 阶段 (CMIP6) 模型预测相结合。

sentences.en: These generally result in positive low-cloud cover (LCC) feedback values with varying magnitudes, meaning they have a warming effect (while negative LCC feedback values mean a cooling effect).
sentences.zh: 这些通常会导致不同幅度的正低云覆盖 (LCC) 反馈值，这意味着它们具有变暖效应（而负 LCC 反馈值意味着降温效应）。

blocks.html_en: The authors of the new study say present-day low-cloud feedback is not often discussed, but prior studies showed that inferred tropical low-cloud feedback could be three to four times smaller when CCF changes are taken from the historical record instead of CMIP6 projections. They say this casts doubt on the plausibility of future cloud feedback estimates based on idealized warming scenarios.

sentences.en: The authors of the new study say present-day low-cloud feedback is not often discussed, but prior studies showed that inferred tropical low-cloud feedback could be three to four times smaller when CCF changes are taken from the historical record instead of CMIP6 projections.
sentences.zh: 这项新研究的作者表示，目前的低云反馈并不经常被讨论，但之前的研究表明，当 CCF 变化取自历史记录而不是 CMIP6 预测时，推断的热带低云反馈可能会小三到四倍。

sentences.en: They say this casts doubt on the plausibility of future cloud feedback estimates based on idealized warming scenarios.
sentences.zh: 他们表示，这让人对基于理想化变暖情景的未来云反馈估计的合理性产生怀疑。

blocks.html_en: Satellite data provide some historical clarity

sentences.en: Satellite data provide some historical clarity
sentences.zh: 卫星数据提供了一些历史清晰度

blocks.html_en: The team combined observations from 13 satellites with atmospheric reanalysis data in an attempt to reconstruct past cloud conditions from 1979 to 2025. To do this, they developed an anomaly-based framework that minimizes platform- and algorithm-dependent biases and provides more consistent comparisons across satellites.

sentences.en: The team combined observations from 13 satellites with atmospheric reanalysis data in an attempt to reconstruct past cloud conditions from 1979 to 2025.
sentences.zh: 该团队将 13 颗卫星的观测结果与大气再分析数据结合起来，试图重建 1979 年至 2025 年过去的云状况。

sentences.en: To do this, they developed an anomaly-based framework that minimizes platform- and algorithm-dependent biases and provides more consistent comparisons across satellites.
sentences.zh: 为此，他们开发了一个基于异常的框架，最大限度地减少平台和算法相关的偏差，并提供跨卫星更一致的比较。

blocks.html_en: Their results showed that tropical low-cloud cover increased by about 0.18% per decade from 1979 to 2025. This rise mainly consisted of stratocumulus clouds, which are especially effective at reflecting sunlight. They also estimated that the rise in cloud cover produced a negative low-cloud feedback of about −0.79 watts per square meter per degree Celsius, a cooling influence that contributed to slowed warming in the past few decades.

sentences.en: Their results showed that tropical low-cloud cover increased by about 0.18% per decade from 1979 to 2025.
sentences.zh: 他们的结果显示，从 1979 年到 2025 年，热带低云覆盖率每十年增加约 0.18%。

sentences.en: This rise mainly consisted of stratocumulus clouds, which are especially effective at reflecting sunlight.
sentences.zh: 这次上升主要由层积云组成，层积云对反射阳光特别有效。

sentences.en: They also estimated that the rise in cloud cover produced a negative low-cloud feedback of about −0.79 watts per square meter per degree Celsius, a cooling influence that contributed to slowed warming in the past few decades.
sentences.zh: 他们还估计，云量的增加产生了每平方米每摄氏度约-0.79瓦的负低云反馈，这种冷却影响导致了过去几十年变暖的减缓。

blocks.html_en: "This overall LCC increase has occurred alongside a ∼1 K rise in global mean surface temperature, linking cloud changes to the observed warming trend. The CCF decomposition indicates that the positive LCC trend is primarily driven by increases in estimated inversion strength (EIS) and surface wind speed contributions (0.14 ± 0.04%/decade and 0.07 ± 0.02%/decade, respectively), while the SST contribution (−0.08 ± 0.02%/decade) acts in the opposite direction and partly offsets the increase," the study authors write.

sentences.en: "This overall LCC increase has occurred alongside a ∼1 K rise in global mean surface temperature, linking cloud changes to the observed warming trend.
sentences.zh: “LCC 总体增加是伴随着全球平均地表温度约 1 K 的上升而发生的，这将云的变化与观测到的变暖趋势联系起来。

sentences.en: The CCF decomposition indicates that the positive LCC trend is primarily driven by increases in estimated inversion strength (EIS) and surface wind speed contributions (0.14 ± 0.04%/decade and 0.07 ± 0.02%/decade, respectively), while the SST contribution (−0.08 ± 0.02%/decade) acts in the opposite direction and partly offsets the increase," the study authors write.
sentences.zh: CCF分解表明，正的LCC趋势主要是由估计逆温强度（EIS）和表面风速贡献（分别为0.14±0.04％/十年和0.07±0.02％/十年）的增加驱动的，而海温贡献（−0.08±0.02％/十年）则相反，并部分抵消了增加，”研究作者写道。

sentences.en: Low-cloud feedback for different scenarios.
sentences.zh: 针对不同场景的低云反馈。

sentences.en: Credit: Geophysical Research Letters (2026).
sentences.zh: 图片来源：地球物理研究快报 (2026)。

blocks.html_en: Combining historical data with climate models

sentences.en: Combining historical data with climate models
sentences.zh: 将历史数据与气候模型相结合

blocks.html_en: Although historical warming shows an association with a negative low-cloud feedback that provides a welcome cooling effect, the team found that when models matched this historical data, the news was still not good. When the team compared the observed historical record with simulations from CMIP6 climate models, they found that the models that best matched observed cloud patterns and trends projected the strongest positive future low-cloud feedback and higher climate sensitivity.

sentences.en: Although historical warming shows an association with a negative low-cloud feedback that provides a welcome cooling effect, the team found that when models matched this historical data, the news was still not good.
sentences.zh: 尽管历史变暖表明与低云负反馈相关，从而提供了可喜的降温效果，但研究小组发现，当模型与历史数据相匹配时，消息仍然不好。

sentences.en: When the team compared the observed historical record with simulations from CMIP6 climate models, they found that the models that best matched observed cloud patterns and trends projected the strongest positive future low-cloud feedback and higher climate sensitivity.
sentences.zh: 当研究小组将观测到的历史记录与 CMIP6 气候模型的模拟进行比较时，他们发现与观测到的云模式和趋势最匹配的模型预测了未来最强烈的低云积极反馈和更高的气候敏感性。

blocks.html_en: In other idealized climate-model experiments that did not match historical observations, the average response showed low-cloud losses and extra warming. This appears to be because historical observations indicate more reflective clouds, particularly in the East Pacific, while idealized warming patterns favored less reflective clouds.

sentences.en: In other idealized climate-model experiments that did not match historical observations, the average response showed low-cloud losses and extra warming.
sentences.zh: 在其他与历史观测结果不符的理想化气候模型实验中，平均响应显示低云损失和额外变暖。

sentences.en: This appears to be because historical observations indicate more reflective clouds, particularly in the East Pacific, while idealized warming patterns favored less reflective clouds.
sentences.zh: 这似乎是因为历史观测表明反射云较多，特别是在东太平洋，而理想化的变暖模式则有利于反射较少的云。

blocks.html_en: Most models predicted more heating in the eastern Pacific, similar to western Pacific regions, but observed patterns instead show that a mix of hot and cool air has allowed for the formation of low-cloud cover. However, scientists predict that further heating and changes in atmospheric circulation will eventually reverse this protective effect.

sentences.en: Most models predicted more heating in the eastern Pacific, similar to western Pacific regions, but observed patterns instead show that a mix of hot and cool air has allowed for the formation of low-cloud cover.
sentences.zh: 大多数模型预测东太平洋的升温程度更高，与西太平洋地区类似，但观察到的模式却表明，冷热空气的混合导致了低云量的形成。

sentences.en: However, scientists predict that further heating and changes in atmospheric circulation will eventually reverse this protective effect.
sentences.zh: 然而，科学家预测，进一步升温和大气环流的变化最终将逆转这种保护作用。

blocks.html_en: While this study contributes cloud feedback estimates that could improve projections of future temperature rise, there is still room for improvement in these models. The authors write, "Improving the representation of SST trends in coupled climate models—and better constraining their forced versus internal components—will therefore be critical for narrowing uncertainties in cloud feedbacks and climate sensitivity."

sentences.en: While this study contributes cloud feedback estimates that could improve projections of future temperature rise, there is still room for improvement in these models.
sentences.zh: 虽然这项研究提供了云反馈估计，可以改善对未来气温上升的预测，但这些模型仍有改进的空间。

sentences.en: The authors write, "Improving the representation of SST trends in coupled climate models—and better constraining their forced versus internal components—will therefore be critical for narrowing uncertainties in cloud feedbacks and climate sensitivity."
sentences.zh: 作者写道：“因此，改善耦合气候模型中海温趋势的表征，并更好地限制其强制成分与内部成分，对于缩小云反馈和气候敏感性的不确定性至关重要。”

content_html_zh:
最近发表在《地球物理研究快报》上的一项研究将历史卫星观测与大气再分析数据结合起来，构建了数十年的低云量记录，希望改进当前的气候模型。

研究表明，低云量历史上对变暖具有保护作用，但气候模型仍然显示，未来低云量的影响会减弱，而且气候敏感性也会增加。

对低云的多云理解

科学家们知道低云会将阳光反射回太空，这会导致冷却效果。 然而，这些云如何受到地球变暖的影响仍存在不确定性。 大多数气候模型预计，随着地球变暖，热带海洋上空的低云会减少，并进一步加剧变暖，但它们可能减少的程度以及对变暖的影响程度因模型而异。

到目前为止，卫星云记录已经支离破碎，因为仪器、校准和云定义随着时间的推移而发生了变化。 这阻碍了对现代变暖时期低云变化程度的可靠直接估计，使得在未来预测中难以对云效应进行建模。

一些研究根据云与云控制因素 (CCF) 之间的关系推断出低云反馈，然后将其与 CMIP 第 6 阶段 (CMIP6) 模型预测相结合。 这些通常会导致不同幅度的正低云覆盖 (LCC) 反馈值，这意味着它们具有变暖效应（而负 LCC 反馈值意味着降温效应）。

这项新研究的作者表示，目前的低云反馈并不经常被讨论，但之前的研究表明，当 CCF 变化取自历史记录而不是 CMIP6 预测时，推断的热带低云反馈可能会小三到四倍。 他们表示，这让人对基于理想化变暖情景的未来云反馈估计的合理性产生怀疑。

卫星数据提供了一些历史清晰度

该团队将 13 颗卫星的观测结果与大气再分析数据结合起来，试图重建 1979 年至 2025 年过去的云状况。 为此，他们开发了一个基于异常的框架，最大限度地减少平台和算法相关的偏差，并提供跨卫星更一致的比较。

他们的结果显示，从 1979 年到 2025 年，热带低云覆盖率每十年增加约 0.18%。 这次上升主要由层积云组成，层积云对反射阳光特别有效。 他们还估计，云量的增加产生了每平方米每摄氏度约-0.79瓦的负低云反馈，这种冷却影响导致了过去几十年变暖的减缓。

“LCC 总体增加是伴随着全球平均地表温度约 1 K 的上升而发生的，这将云的变化与观测到的变暖趋势联系起来。 CCF分解表明，正的LCC趋势主要是由估计逆温强度（EIS）和表面风速贡献（分别为0.14±0.04％/十年和0.07±0.02％/十年）的增加驱动的，而海温贡献（−0.08±0.02％/十年）则相反，并部分抵消了增加，”研究作者写道。

将历史数据与气候模型相结合

尽管历史变暖表明与低云负反馈相关，从而提供了可喜的降温效果，但研究小组发现，当模型与历史数据相匹配时，消息仍然不好。 当研究小组将观测到的历史记录与 CMIP6 气候模型的模拟进行比较时，他们发现与观测到的云模式和趋势最匹配的模型预测了未来最强烈的低云积极反馈和更高的气候敏感性。

在其他与历史观测结果不符的理想化气候模型实验中，平均响应显示低云损失和额外变暖。 这似乎是因为历史观测表明反射云较多，特别是在东太平洋，而理想化的变暖模式则有利于反射较少的云。

大多数模型预测东太平洋的升温程度更高，与西太平洋地区类似，但观察到的模式却表明，冷热空气的混合导致了低云量的形成。 然而，科学家预测，进一步升温和大气环流的变化最终将逆转这种保护作用。

虽然这项研究提供了云反馈估计，可以改善对未来气温上升的预测，但这些模型仍有改进的空间。 作者写道：“因此，改善耦合气候模型中海温趋势的表征，并更好地限制其强制成分与内部成分，对于缩小云反馈和气候敏感性的不确定性至关重要。”

本文由我们的作者 Krystal Kasal 为您撰写，由 Lisa Lock 编辑，并由 Robert Egan 进行事实检查和审阅——本文是人类精心工作的结果。 我们依靠像您这样的读者来保持独立科学新闻的活力。 如果此报告对您很重要，请考虑捐款（尤其是每月）。 作为感谢，您将获得一个无广告帐户。
